things.name: Museum of Modern and Contemporary Art
things.description: One of France's finest modern art museums with a world-class collection including works by Leger, Warhol and key French Modernists in a bold modern building

things.name: Mine-Image Museum
things.description: Underground coal mining museum descending into the real galleries of the Couriot colliery; one of France's most dramatic industrial heritage experiences

things.name: Museum of Art and Industry
things.description: Museum exploring Saint-Etienne's three great industries: arms manufacture, the ribbon trade and cycling; includes the world's finest collection of bicycles

things.name: Biennale Internationale Design
things.description: Saint-Etienne is France's first UNESCO City of Design; the biennial design festival transforms the city into a global showcase for contemporary industrial design

things.name: Cite du Design
things.description: International design school and exhibition complex with a spectacular glass structure cantilevered over the restored 19th-century arms factory

things.name: Saint-Etienne Cathedral
things.description: 15th-century Gothic cathedral in the old town with a striking west facade, notable stained glass and the Bishop's Palace forming an imposing ensemble

things.name: Place du Peuple
things.description: The main square of Saint-Etienne with its large covered market, surrounded by cafes and the daily bustle of a working industrial city

things.name: Planete Aquatique
things.description: Large water park and leisure complex near Lyon, accessible from Saint-Etienne for a family day out with indoor and outdoor pools and water slides

things.name: Gorges de la Loire
things.description: Spectacular volcanic gorge carved by the Loire south of Saint-Etienne with hiking trails, rock climbing and the Grangent dam lake for water sports

things.name: Stade Geoffroy-Guichard
things.description: The iconic Chaudron stadium, home of AS Saint-Etienne since 1931 with a 41,965 capacity; the most successful club in French football history with 10 league titles
